description_long: This is a military-style, twin-engine propeller airplane with an olive-green fuselage and distinct black-and-white cross insignias on the wings and tail, reminiscent of World War II German aircraft. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, with two visible engines mounted on the leading edge, each equipped with a three-blade propeller. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a clear canopy and multiple window panels. The tail section features a conventional single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with a swastika insignia on the tail fin. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable. The surface finish is matte, and the airplane's overall design is streamlined for aerodynamic efficiency.
description: Olive-green, twin-propeller military airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, German WWII insignias, and a streamlined, enclosed cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin propellers, nose, and symmetrical wing layout.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the cockpit, engine nacelles, and tail insignia.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, cross insignias, and streamlined fuselage.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the other side of the fuselage, engines, and tail details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable based on design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-panel canopy.
